reasoning: The bank reserves have been on a slight downward trend over the previous several months as QT continues, although the rate of decline has moderated somewhat. Recent weekly changes have mostly been negative (4 out of the last 5 weeks down), pointing to a sub-50% chance of an increase in the very short term (Feb 22). However, historical data from last year indicates a seasonal tendency for cash assets to rise slightly from February into March (e.g., 3285 in Feb 2025 rising to 3324 in March 2025). Taking into account the negative near-term momentum but adjusting for increased uncertainty and possible seasonal upward pressure over the longer horizon (which anchors expectations closer to 50% for the distant date), I estimate 0.42 for one week ahead and 0.47 for one month ahead.
